mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with a falling FEDFUNDS rate, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration signals weakening household income and demand, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day forward returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is unstable across two consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and labor income, which should lift near-term corporate revenues and support positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal is not stronger than either series alone, or if instances with both series rising fail to show positive 20-day returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior slowdown marks an easing of supply constraints and higher real activity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum episodes do not outperform non-triggered periods on 5-day forward returns, or if the signal flips negative in more than one rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL combined with elevated FEDFUNDS tightens real financial conditions and compresses equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-policy signal does not predict worse 60-day returns than either CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone, or if positive returns dominate during high-inflation/high-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, falling unemployment alongside accelerating payroll growth signals resilient income and spending, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining UNRATE and rising PAYEMS do not outperform baseline on 20-day returns, or if the interaction loses sign consistency in a rolling 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST after prior stabilization indicates housing-demand weakness and future consumption softness, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing contraction episodes fail to generate negative 60-day returns on average, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the previous quarter's market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while RSAFS is also expanding, the economy is translating output growth into realized spending, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales expansion does not outperform single-series signals on 60-day returns, or if the joint condition yields negative median returns in any two consecutive expansion periods.
